description.content: Catalogue browse, search, batch lookup, download metadata, and usage type information.

description.content: Lean-back, ruleset-governed (DMCA, GVL) radio listening sessions.

Create an Interactive Radio listening session from one or more playlists. A listening session is required per user, each time they launch a station. It governs which tracks can be played and tracks the skip budget within the configured ruleset (e.g. DMCA, GVL).

response.name: A listening session.
response.body:
{
  "sessionId": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
  "userId": "500123",
  "ruleset": "dmca",
  "skipsRemaining": 6,
  "createdAt": "2026-05-28T14:30:00Z"
}

description.content: Send the user's playback events. These events influence which tracks are allowed to be played and skipped in the current listening session.
Stream / preview / subscription play reporting required for licensor royalty calculations.
Report a track that has been streamed to a user listening in a radio-style playback. Streams should be logged in near real time, or the same day in batch.
Report a preview clip that has been played. Acceptable usage is 5 POST requests per second with batches of up to 500 logs per request.
Report a track that has been streamed by a subscriber. Logs should be sent in near real-time, or the same day in batches of 10–20 logs per request.
HLS and HTTP Progressive streaming for previews, catalogue, locker and subscriber playback.
Stream a track preview clip (HTTP Progressive). All preview clip plays must be reported via the preview logging endpoint.

Stream any catalogue track available in the user's region (HLS). Designed for non-subscription radio-style plays. Tracks can be cached in an encrypted format and all plays must be reported via the catalogue logging endpoint.
HTTP Progressive variant of catalogue streaming. Same usage rules as the HLS variant.
Stream a previously purchased track from a user's locker (HLS). Supports online plays and offline caching.
HTTP Progressive variant of locker streaming.
Stream a track to a valid subscriber (HLS). Requires an active subscription in the user's region. When a clientId is supplied, concurrent multi-device playback is blocked.

query.description: Identifier of the device requesting playback (used for concurrency control).

description.content: HTTP Progressive variant of subscription streaming.

Bulk-cache a subscription track for offline playback (HLS). Requires the device to be pre-authorised via the offline streaming endpoint. All offline plays must be reported when connectivity is restored.
HTTP Progressive variant of offline subscription streaming.
Authorise, list, and inspect devices registered for offline subscription playback.
Authorise a device for offline subscription streaming. POST body can be JSON or XML.

description.content: Log a sale of a track or release for the specified user and add it to their locker. Currency must match the one provided for the content (typically USD). Returns XML responses only.

urlencoded.description: (Required) ISO 4217 currency code matching the supplied wholesale price.
(Required) Retail price charged to the user.

response.name: Sale logged.

description.content: (Required)
(Required) ISO 4217 currency code matching the supplied wholesale price.
(Required) Retail price charged to the user.

response.body: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

description.content: Remove a purchase of a release from the sales report when the purchase has been refunded.
Remove a purchase of a track from the sales report when the purchase has been refunded.
Notify the platform of subscription state — required for royalty + entitlement.

Create a user's subscription. The platform must be notified of all new subscriptions to grant streaming access and to support royalty and usage reporting. Subscriptions last for at most one month and must be renewed each cycle.

urlencoded.description: (Required) Subscription expiry. Maximum one month from startDate.

response.name: The new subscription.

description.content: (Required)
(Required)
(Required)
(Required) Subscription expiry. Maximum one month from startDate.

  "endDate": "2026-06-28"

description.content: Bulk media transfer for content delivery / catalogue ingestion partners.

Retrieve media files for any track — enables the download of an entire licensed catalogue in any supported format/bitrate. Acceptable usage is 50 requests per second. Use of this endpoint requires additional licensor due diligence.

The modern 7digital (now branded MassiveMusic) Streaming Platform API covering Catalogue, Streaming (HLS + HTTP Progressive), Interactive Radio, Logging, Playlists, Sales/Subscriptions, User Management and offline device authorisation. All operations are signed with OAuth 1.0 against a partner consumer key issued under a commercial agreement.

Contact Support:
 Name: MassiveMusic Client Success
